_**User (2026-03-26 18:49:29Z)**_

jai pas compris ce que je dois faire

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

Tu dois juste mettre à jour les règles de sécurité dans **Firebase Console** pour autoriser la sauvegarde des projets. Voici comment faire :

1. Va sur **[console.firebase.google.com](https://console.firebase.google.com)**
2. Ouvre ton projet **craft-genius-201e6**
3. Dans le menu à gauche → **Firestore Database** → onglet **Rules**
4. **Remplace tout** le contenu par le texte ci-dessous et clique sur **Publish** :

```
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {

    match /users/{uid} {
      allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == uid;
    }

    match /users/{uid}/modProjects/{projectId} {
      allow read, write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == uid;
    }

    match /comments/{document=**} {
      allow read: if true;
      allow create: if request.resource.data.text.size() > 0 && 
                       request.resource.data.name != null;
      allow delete: if request.auth != null;
      allow update: if request.auth != null && 
                       request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;
    }

    match /aiUsage/{uid} {
      allow read: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == uid;
      allow write: if request.auth != null && 
                      request.auth.uid == uid &&
                      request.resource.data.count is int &&
                      request.resource.data.count >= 0 &&
                      request.resource.data.count <= 10 &&
                      request.resource.data.month is string;
    }

    match /siteSettings/{document} {
      allow read, write: if true;
    }
  }
}
```

C'est tout. Ensuite, quand tu crées un projet dans l'éditeur avec ton compte connecté, ça se sauvegarde automatiquement dans Firebase.

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The user is asking about an error. Looking at the attached `mod-editor.js` file, I can see the problem clearly: the file has **duplicate code**. The new Firebase version was prepended, but the old localStorage-only version was appended at the bottom. This causes multiple issues:

1. `const` declarations are duplicated (e.g., `btnNewProject`, `btnImport`, etc.) — this causes a `SyntaxError: Identifier 'btnNewProject' has already been declared`
2. Functions like `nameToId`, `updateIdPreview`, `saveProject`, `deleteProject`, `renderRecentList`, `showToast`, `escHtml` are declared twice


3. The DOM content loaded event listener is also firing twice, which means initialization logic runs multiple times

The fix is straightforward: I need to remove the entire duplicate section at the bottom of the file that contains the old localStorage-only implementation.

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6)**_

The user is seeing "âœ•" instead of "✕" in the delete button. This is a UTF-8 encoding issue - the file is being read/written with incorrect encoding. The character "✕" (U+2715, MULTIPLICATION X) is being stored as its UTF-8 bytes but displayed as Latin-1/Windows-1252, causing "âœ•" to appear.

Let me look at the actual content in the file to understand what's happening.
